title: Linsoul 7Hz Salnotes Zero HiFi 10mm Dynamic Driver in-Ear Earphone IEM with Metal Composite Diaphragm Stainless Steel Faceplate Detachable 2Pin OFC Cable (Blue, with Mic, Type C)
features: 【𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐨𝐥𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝟏𝟎𝐦𝐦 𝐃𝐲𝐧𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐃𝐫𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐫 𝐈𝐄𝐌】The 7Hz Salnotes Zero is a revolutionary earphone to be fitted with a 10mm dynamic driver with metal composite diaphragm. The metallic composite diaphragm used by 7Hz Zero is made of high quality materials, making it easier to resonate or vibrate along with sound waves. It was constructed with ease of use in mind as each part has been designed with precision accuracy so that they will not hinder audio transmission.

【𝐄𝐫𝐠𝐨𝐧𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐒𝐡𝐚𝐩𝐞&𝐌𝐞𝐭𝐚𝐥 𝐇𝐨𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠】7Hz Salnotes Zero is housed in an environment-friendly plastic chassis with a stainless steel faceplate. High precision and precision measurements based on ergonomics were taken to help create an earphone that offers excellent acoustic performance, as well as safety, comfort, and durability.

【𝐃𝐞𝐭𝐚𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞 𝐇𝐢𝐠𝐡 𝐏𝐮𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐲 𝐎𝐅𝐂 𝐂𝐚𝐛𝐥𝐞】The earphones include a detachable cable with gold plated 0.78mm 2Pin connectors. The cable is made of 4 core high purity Oxygen Free Copper in a parallel structure while each core is made of 19*0.08 wires+250D to maximally avoid signal loss and ensure a high fidelity transmission

【𝐍𝟓𝟐 𝐌𝐚𝐠𝐧𝐞𝐭, 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐞 𝐓𝐮𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠】7Hz Salnote ZERO makes the ultimate audio experience available to you from the convenience of your mobile device, laptop, or desktop computer. The fine-tuning technique and use of a dynamic driver ensure accurate and efficient transmission of audio signals. You can enjoy a smooth and accurate sound without any distortion.

【𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐃𝐞𝐩𝐭𝐡 𝐨𝐟 𝐙𝐄𝐑𝐎】By naming it ZERO, we intended to deliver our philosophy of life to every customer. Previously our team has brought Timeless and Eternal to the community, now it’s time for ZERO. ZERO means eternity and mortality without ultimateness. ZERO provides limitless imagination and allows boundless possibilities. Also, ZERO is manufactured in more than one color. Three technicolored colors are adopted for this model to bring you a vibrant atmosphere and rejuvenate your music experience.

reviews.title: The Moondrop Chu is dead. Long live the new king.
reviews.text:
Well the Moondrop Chu's reign didn't last long. These WILDLY outclass everything under $50. They wildly outclass a lot of stuff under $200.

For starters, detachable cable. I hate the fixed cable on the moondrop chu's with a passion - it's all silicone rubbery and tangles, doesn't stay in place, doesn't stay tied up in your pocket or backpack. The chu's come with a microphone, which is the only thing it has going for it, but if you REALLY need a cable, these are still a far better deal with another $10 spent on the cable. This cable is nice, comfortable, wraps up easily around 3-4 fingers and stays coiled properly.

Then, eartips. The Chu's "springtips" don't really fit quite a few people, myself included. They were so bad that the chu's sounded downright broken until I got some better tips on them. The Zero's come with about five different size of tips that were no problem to find a comfortable seal/fit.

Comfort and build wise, they're awesome. My ears can be quite picky, and they basically disappear in your head. They're light and smooth plastic, with a nice stainless steel plate on the outside. No complaints, they just work perfectly as IEM's and feel plenty durable to just live in your backpack.

Sound wise, these things really up the game. The Chu's have excellent technical performance (detail is great) but they're downright claustrophobic. It feels like every instrument in the band is licking the inside of your ear. And it's unpleasant. They're tuned quite neutral, but with no sense of space, instrument separation or imaging (that sense of more than left/right, but front/back and in between) they're just not a pleasant sound. The Zero's have IMPRESSIVE separation and imaging. Not impressive at $20. Impressive for IEM's, period. There's $300-500 stuff that doesn't do imaging and soundstage this well.

The bass could be cleaner and it doesn't quite hit the detail of the chu's (which are also punching well into the $500 range in that one specific area) but I'm nitpicking HARD. For $20, these have no right sounding so spacious, detailed (you can enjoy the detail a lot more when the instruments don't feel all laid on top of each other) and pleasant. They're not overly bassy, but they are thumpier than the chu's. No overly sparkly either. Just a very "grown up" tuning meant to make music sound nice, paired with truly outstanding soundstage.

If you play video games, there's nothing anywhere near these in the price range. You'll hear sounds ahead, behind, and how far away they are.

Songs with real detail and separation will dance around you (Bobby McFerrin, Polyphia) and show you things you didn't even know were in your music.

The only next step up I can think of from these is the $50 Truthear zero's (same model name, different brand) but they're only marginally better (with a slightly worse fit) and the $100 7hz diokos are a very noticeable step up in every way. But jeez. These things are $20. I don't understand how they're so good for that little money.

Anyone else entering the $20-$50 space better bring their A+ game. These are a tough act to follow. If you really need a microphone, these are still unbeatable with another $10-15 for a cable with one.

reviews.title: Fantastic First IEM w/ Insane Value
reviews.text:
These were my first entry into IEMs and I really enjoyed the experience. Coming from Google Pixel Buds Pros, these were a significant improvement in sound quality but given the abundance of excellent budget headphones in the chi-fi market, I can't comment as to whether they're better than any of the others you'd find for a similar price.

I also really like the USB-C 2-pin cable that it comes with. It has a subtler finish compared to twisted cables and it also has a built-in mic that works well enough for calls. I actually bought a higher end set of IEMS and ended up using the Salnotes' cable since similar cables cost 1-2x the price of the Salnotes themselves.

reviews.title: nice very good price
reviews.text: love em ty

reviews.title: TYPE C EARBUDS!!!!
reviews.text:
They're the best quality out of all the type c buds I found on here.

Been looking for a while and found these. Before, I had a type c to 3.5 cable which kept getting in the way and having audio issues. Then got a type c adapter to 3.5 and the same thing happened. With this, its easy. Cable still gets in the way, but no audio issues.

Theyre really good earbuds. Not some 100 plus IEMS, but still great for price and sound quality. Overall these are a steal and really helpful if you haven't fallen into the wireless scam where they forced you to buy wireless earbuds by removing the jack.

Prefer cable over wireless. No brain fry.

reviews.text: Wow, incredible sound. this IEM is a must-have for anyone into music or new to Hi-Res, or just enjoying everyday listening, this is for you. the sound that it produces from one driver is incredible, but what makes this even greater is the price, you just can't go wrong for $20. buy it, test it, and you won't regret it.

reviews.title: Great Headset at a Cheap Price

Excellent hands free headset that work great with the Avaya model 9608G phone.
Based on the cheap price, I was not anticipating something that worked this good.
The overall construction is good and the sound quality at both ends is good as well.
Since purchasing the first set, we have purchased several more sets for others in our office. All of which have worked great. Just plug then into the headset jack on the back of the phone and you are ready to go, nothing more to do.
It would have been nice if they offered a headset dual headphones to block out more background noise. I did inquire, but they don't.
Overall, myself and others using them in our office, are happy with them.

No pairing required, no lip-sync delay, no need to be facing a certain angle or position, and no interference when someone passes by.

description:
Features
These BLLQ Anti-Slip Ear Hooks hold your Air Pods securely and prevents it from falling out. Great for running, jogging, cycling, gym and other fitness activities!
Fit &Comfortable
Ear Hooks are made from High Quality Hybrid Thermoplastic polyurethane(TPU) to ensure that they keep their shape perfectly fit for your ear and Air Pods, they are very useful to prevent earbuds falling out and very comfortable for use.
Easy Installation
Easy installation & hassle-free removal. Put the Air Pods on the Earhooks and it loops over the ear.
Durable
These Ear Hooks don’t fit in charge case, Please take if off before put into the charge case. But Don’t Worry: These High Quality Ear Hooks is very durable that they can be frequently put on and removed and would not be break or out of shape.
Package includes:
BLLQ Anti-Slip Ear Hooks 1 Pair (Left & Right 2 PCS), Blue cleaning cloth with BLLQ LOGO 1PCS,(Hooks Only ,Earphones not included).

Great fit and manufacture. And the finish on the silicone is easily the softest and most even I've even seen/felt. It's so soft. I find myself mindlessly picking it up while sitting at my computer and running my fingers over the surface turning it in my hand because it feels so nice. Elago knocked it out of the park again.

reviews.title: Handy

reviews.title: Neckbands are fine, but earhooks are useless for me
reviews.text:
The neckbands worked fine, and all items gripped my gen 2 AirPods okay, but the earhooks were not even a little useful for me.

I’m used to wearing headphones with integral earhooks, and loved the fit of those, but these add-on earhooks for Airpods do not have the right amount of rigidity, nor is it easy enough to rotate and position them appropriately with respect to the AirPod stem so that the combined geometry of the two would ever align to my ear. Because of those two factors, they simultaneously made my AirPods look kind of stupid while at the same time actually making the fit/hold even worse; they seemed almost more likely to fall out with the hooks than without them.

reviews.title: Poor quality silicone that easily tears
reviews.text: I didn't know that silicone could actually tear like this stuff has when I removed them from my AirPods. I'm past the return window and these literally ripped after about a month and a half of use when I was trying to slide them off the AirPods. Look elsewhere!

title: Dekoni Audio Memory Foam Replacement Ear Pads Compatible with Sennheiser HD800 and HD800S Headphones (Elite Velour)
features: Designed and Tuned for the Sennheiser HD800 and HD800S

reviews.title: Comfortable fit
reviews.text:
They snap on easily. They are a little too thick for my taste though. You almost forget you're wearing the HD800's with the stock ear pads because of how light and thin they are. With the Dekoni's you've got memory foam cushions pushing on the sides of your head the entire time, reminding you of the giant cans on your head. They are even thicker than the pads on my HD820's. Price is a little steep but only $5 more than stock pads.

The materials feel like nice quality, similar to my HD820's. They're just not as comfortable though, in the sense that you don't forget you're wearing them like you do with the stock pads. If you have the patience, I think it's better to order genuine replacement pads from SoundSpares and just wait for them to ship from the UK. Considering the stock pads are about the same price, I think they're the better option.

reviews.title: Comfortable, not a fan of how the sound changes
reviews.text:
More comfortable than the OEM pads. Does changes the soundstage a bit while also changing the sound signature. I do notice the increase in sub-bass and increased midrange as stated in the description of these pads, but not sure if I like the trade-off of a reduced soundstage. For me, the reduced soundstage makes it sounds a bit muffled, and since I like the clear/clean sound of the basic earpads I'm not really a fan of the changes. But I guess if you want to change the sound of your hd800 then it could be good.

[EDIT] I chose to cut off the cover of the pads and used the base HD800 covers instead. I actually like it more. I wouldn't recommend others to cut their pads though as it's irreversible.

reviews.title: A no-brainer improvement for the HD800
reviews.text:
These replace the foam ear pads from your Sennheiser HD800 phones - and they're an obvious purchase for fans of these headphones. It comes delivered in a protective, flip-open box, and the pads themselves are soft and comfortable. It's every bit as nice-fitting as the original pads. But most users will probably need to do a few things to perfect the sound.

1. Adjust the EQ. If you use an HD800, you probably use either an amp or some kind of EQ setup. When you use these pads the first time, you may notice that certain music is too bassy, or has too much treble. You can easily change the sound signature by playing around with the EQ. You'll find that the bass has more presence with the Dekoni pads, but I didn't notice much change with the treble. If, however, you don't use an amp or adjust the EQ, the sound will sound better with the stock Sennheiser pads.

2. The "sticking point." Sennheiser's stock pads are soft and resilient, and designed especially for the HD800. The Dekoni pads sound equally good, but some might find the sheepskin a little "tacky" at first. (I got used to it pretty quickly.) Also, the Dekoni pads are notably more warm-feeling than the stock pads. If either of these are a problem for you, you can upgrade to the Dekoni "fenestrated" pads - or just use the Sennheiser pads.

3. Looks. No question, the leather pads are notably attractive - a real improvement over stock.

4. Installation. While Dekoni includes a plastic tool to help pry the pads, you would be well advised to watch a few videos beforehand. Dekoni didn't include an installation guide.

These pads are a no-brainer improvement over stock.

reviews.title: OK Headphones
reviews.text: I think these headphones are more like 3.5 stars but I rounded up.  I have kind of a small head for an adult female, and still these headphones were too tight.  Usually I can wear headphones made for kids but not these.  These are definitely for smaller heads and small ears since the ear cups get painful after a while since they aren't big enough to fully cover my ears.  The sound is o.k., nothing to write home about, but for the price (as of this review these head phones cost $19.44) I think they are worth it.  Great for kids, not so good for adults, even if you are a pinhead.

reviews.title: High quality headphones
reviews.text: These are perfect for my 4th grade child to use at school. It is hard to find headphones with plug-ins and that aren't wireless. For my son, the ones with a plug are best to use his school issued ipad. These are sturdy and I'm hoping they last the entire school year.

reviews.title: Not really for large heads.
reviews.text: All the talk about "classroom" and "child's" in the Amazon listing really means "this is a small headset." It's not big enough for me. I have a large (7 3/4) head and this thing actually hurts my ears (presses on them too hard). If you need headphones for a child then this would work just fine. Not for me.

So like...yeah it works well, good range but the touch activated button was a big no from me.

title: Audio Transmitter Receiver TV Computer Speaker Car Adapter 3.5mm Bluetooth 5.0 2-in-1 for Home Sound System/TV/PC/Tablet Headphone General (Black) (Large)
description:
Switch mode:
(1) RX: Receive mode, indicator: blue LED  (2) TX: Transmit mode, indicator light: red LED
RX mode:
Pairing name: KN321, find KN321 in the Bluetooth settings of the phone to pair. The blue light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the blue light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
how to use:
(1) Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio input of the speaker and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)confirm that it is in RX mode (blue light flash). Find the pairing name in the Bluetooth settings of the phone: KN321 click to pair.
TX mode:
TX mode will automatically search for other Bluetooth receiving devices (such as Bluetooth headset, Bluetooth speaker, Bluetooth receiver, etc.), The red light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the red light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
How to use:
(1)Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio output of the devices and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)Confirm that it is in TX mode (red light flash). The KN321 will automatically search for Bluetooth headset or Bluetooth speaker pairing.
features: ★[BLUETOOTH TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER 2IN1] Receiver mode: wirelessly streams audio from your Bluetooth-enabled phone, tablet, or computer to your wired stereo, speakers, or headphones (Only support to be paired with one Bluetooth receiver). Transmitter mode: wirelessly streams audio from your non-Bluetooth TV, home stereo system, or CD player to your Bluetooth headphones or speakers. Switch between transmitter and receiver modes with the simple flick of a switch.
★[NO DRIVER REQUIRED PLUG READY TO USE] No need to download driver, plug and play TV computer or Bluetooth speaker headset.

★[PORTABLE AUDIO-STREAMING POWER] Bluetooth Adapter has got powerful wireless audio streaming capabilities. It features Bluetooth 5.0 chip to ensure high fidelity transmission of Bluetooth audio signal for music streaming to those speakers or home stereo systems that doesn’t have the capability.Slip it into your backpack or laptop bag and take it on-the-go without the slightest effort.

★[WIDE APPLICATION] With the included 3.5mm cable and 3.5mm to 2RCA cable, this Receiver Transmitter adapter can widely be used on your computer, laptop, home stereo system, headphone, smartphone, Mp3 player, CD player, Cars with AUX-in and 3.5mm connector/ Computer Speakers/ Odinary Speakers/ Subwoofers/ Radios/ Multimedia Speakers/ Power Amplifiers/ Echo Walls, etc.

★[QUALITY ASSURANCE AND SERVICE] Provides a 60-day return and 12-month replacement warranty, as well as lifetime accessible technical support. Please contact us if you have any questions. Reply to your message within 12 hours.

reviews.title: Great quality device if you're on a budget
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:e49e95641bb1dc426258e889516d11e0]] Over great product for the price. I can't complain. If you've got the money and would like to go for a more "high end" product there are plenty of options available. However, this one appeals greater to the general public. Most reviews say the same thing: It cuts out if too far away and it has a short battery life. Also if it is being used while charging you'll heard a little buzzing noise. You get what you pay for. It does its job and I can't complain  for the price.

reviews.title: For A/V wireless solution, it is workable but not perfect.
reviews.text:
First, my purpose is to stream audio to wired QC headphones from my AV receiver, as a replacement for my broken analog FM TV sound transmitter/phones. For THIS purpose... I bought a pair of these BT devices, and it works, but the sync between audio and video is - versus analog - perceptible. The streaming generation is less sensitive to this, but AV-philes find it annoying.

It is a problem in all BT, compressed audio, technologies - to some degree. Compressed audio requires buffering, buffering introduces some minimum delay, and the delay can be worse depending upon the nature of the audio.
For many programs (sports, etc) sync isn't a problem, but in close-up talky-talky scenes the audio lag is annoying vs. the FM phones, which have imperceptible lag. For NON-SYNCed applications - this is a non-issue.
I wish Roku had a AV sync setting (+/- ms). This is what BT 5.0 does - it delays the video based on the estimated audio lag. *But that doesn't help here because these devices do NOT control the video stream*.

Otherwise, audio quality of BT vs FM is equivalent. My FM phones were very good (AR, $150) and audio was excellent, so the BT pair has good sound in this application. I have not noted any static others reported, except for a slight hum when the RX is not connected. Generally, the BT audio is equivalent to any other BT audio connection (ie., streaming iPhone to car stereo, etc.).
8/18/2020 Revision: After a power outage, I noticed a VERY bad hum. Removed RX side's power, no change. Removed TX sides power (from USB outlet on cable box), NO HUM. I tried several other USB outlets, each had some hum though with different qualities. Finally, I got out a good high amp USB hub & used it to power the TX side - no hum at all. SO... the transmitting side is VERY sensitive to ripple in its USB power circuitry.

One VERY annoying thing - my devices both power off after 4hrs (6hrs?) regardless of input, even though both TX/RX devices are permanently wired to USB power. It ought to power down only if on battery or unconnected for some time (firmware update perhaps?). Other than this obvious design choice, I have had no connection issues - power on TX, then RX, connects reliably in ~10s. Once connected stays reliably connected UNTIL it powers down.

So the good: Cheap solution to use superior wired headphones. Small & USB powered, so hides away easily on both TX and RX sides. Cheaper & more versatile than quality FM TV transmitter/phone combos.

And the bad: Audio LAGS behind video noticeably, 50-150ms. Also, I haven't tried with multiple BT sources - but there is no UI, no way to select a specific source - I suspect this would be a problem for some.

Overall: For the price, I can't complain and wouldn't spend 5x for good FM TV audio phones.

reviews.title: Compatible With All Devices
reviews.text: Amazing product! I like the easy connectivity and sound quality is top of the line. I wish had known about this product before. It is compatible with all of my devices. Even my older television! Five stars all the way!

reviews.title: Nope. Didn't work with TV..
reviews.text: I bought this to try to move the sound FROM my Panasonic, non-bluetooth television TO my bluetooth headphones or a bluetooth speaker. Tried both, neither worked - was a complete waste of time. Now, who's going to refund me for the printer ink to print a label to return it?

reviews.title: Works like a charm
reviews.text: Fantastic. Works right out of the box. I set it up with an aux to an older stereo and it connected right away. I'm probably going to buy another one for the office.

title: RedSkyPower 20 Pack Multi Color Kid's Wired Earphone Headphones, Individually Bagged, Disposable Earphones Ideal for Students in Classroom Libraries Schools, Bulk Wholesale
description: Redskypower is a global manufacturer of wholesale headphones. Kids Headphones from our specialized line will immerse the wearer in the audio experience from the minute they are placed on. Just what you need is here. Lovely earbuds - Kids will immediately become immersed in the audio experience once it is turned on. In order to prevent hearing loss or damage, the Redskypower kids headset is also constrained by sound-control circuitry to create a sound that is no louder than 85dB. Package: Each earbud is packaged separately. 3.5mm L-shaped audio jack connectors. Wired communication. Stereo in-ear earbud headphones 32 OHM Impedance 5 mW is the maximum input power 85db Sensitivity (front) Response to Frequency: 20 Hz to 20 kHz Plug size: 3.5mm stereo. Suitable for the majority of smartphones, a set of headphones for the entire class, as well as Mac, PC, Android, fitness, and audio equipment. Easily fits in your pocket, bag, or backpack. Excellent sound quality across the board. Strong and Simple to Carry - Better for frequent usage in classrooms, libraries, and other educational settings. Design for In Ear - Incredibly light, cozy, smooth, and clear sound quality for a cozy listening experience
features: Bulk Earphones 20 Pack, Mixed 10 Colors, Each Individually OPP Packaged, Red, Pink, Blue, Green, Purple, Orange, Yellow, Gray, White and Black.
The ideal set of headphones for classes and students. Earbuds that cost less than $1 per piece and have stereo sound that is clean and balanced.
The headphones' 4-foot wire and 3.5mm L shaped plug make them compatible with iPad, Chromebooks, and PCs.
Excellent for classrooms, schools, libraries, museums, test centers, and shared spaces.

Redskypower warranty: If your product is damaged within three months of the order, we'll provide a free replacement. Redskypower satisfaction: If you're not completely satisfied with your product, we'll issue a full refund.

reviews.title: Cheap Buys Cheap
reviews.text: Not sure what I expected for so little, so shame on me. I'll tell you these made my 9-year-old's ears hurt. I'm not sure how they could cancel any noise, as there is no buffer. I tried the ones she put on and they are very uncomfortable. Returning.

reviews.title: Great product and great price.
reviews.text: We bought a pack of these for my son because he goes through headphones crazy fast; since we bought these, he's still on the first pair. We will be buying more in the future if we have to.

reviews.title: They are priced low enough that a defective or marginal one doesn't sour the price.
reviews.text: Quality control could be a little better, but the price for the multipack is tough to beat.

reviews.title: For the cost… I am super happy with them.
reviews.text: I’m giving these earbuds five stars because they exceed my expectations when they only cost seventy cents each. I find it hard to complain about these, especially considering the cost. The sound is quite adequate for such earbuds. I use these in a classroom setting for kids who either cannot afford earbuds or forget there is at home. They are individually packaged. Nice assortment of colors. Though they are being sold by a US company, I do not show where they are made. I can only assume they are made in China.

reviews.title: Great Sound Just Wish they had a Microphone
reviews.text: Fun colors and love this type of ear plug over the cone shaped style. Listening to music on my phone sounds great  but I wish they had a microphone so I could easily answer calls. Now I have to unplug the earphones when the phone starts to ring and then re-plug after I'm done on the call. I guess they are really designed for use on exclusive music players. The listing is confusing because it says "Bluetooth" which they don't have, but the wire is about 4ft. long so your music unit can easily be kept anywhere convenient. No volume control either so that has to be handled on your phone/music player.
